channel.title: Pve on Steve Dong - A System Administrator's Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Pve on Steve Dong - A System Administrator's Blog

item.title: Install Proxmox Ve on Debian 9
item.description: Install Proxmox on Debian 9 via UTSC Mirrors in china Change your ip and hostname Chang IP ADDRESS Change your IP address to static. ``` bash # This file describes the network interfaces available on your system # and how to activate them. For more information, see interfaces(5). source /etc/network/interfaces.d/* # The loopback network interface auto lo iface lo inet loopback # The primary network interface #allow-hotplug eno1 auto eno1 #iface eno1 inet dhcp iface eno1 inet static address *.

item.title: Proxmox VE 5.x Installation
item.description: Proxmox notebook 01 Proxmox won’t install- “A volume group called pve already exists Solution Boot the proxmox installer USB Select Debug Mode Press “CTRL+D” to start the installer wizard. Click on abort Run “vgdisplay” to dislay the LVM PG’s. Run “vgremode pve ” to remove pve PG. Remove “No valid Subscription” Message sed -i.bak "s/data.status !== 'Active'/false/g" /usr/share/javascript/proxmox-widget-toolkit/proxmoxlib.js && systemctl restart pveproxy.service or change it manually.
